mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in retail sales (RSAFS) signal robust consumer spending, which drives corporate revenue and earnings growth, pushing S&P 500 prices higher over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negatively correlated with RSAFS level or change over any rolling 3-year period, or Rank IC remains below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A decrease of at least 25 basis points in the federal funds rate over a single month represents an unexpected easing that lowers discount rates and boosts equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS decreases by 25bp or more in a month
failure_condition: In more than 40% of trigger events over a 5-year window, 5-day forward returns are negative or zero.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicate a tightening labor market that fuels consumption and corporate earnings, driving S&P 500 gains over a 60-trading-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change > 0 and UNRATE month-over-month change < 0
failure_condition: Joint positive PAYEMS and negative UNRATE changes fail to produce positive median 60-day forward returns, or the signal's win rate is below 55% over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During economic recessions (negative GDP growth), a month-over-month increase in industrial production (INDPRO) signals the start of a recovery, which is rewarded by positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During NBER-defined recessions, INDPRO increases are followed by negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and erodes real earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-trading-day window.
failure_condition: The correlation between CPIAUCSL level and subsequent 20-day returns is positive over any 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC is above -0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate of at least 0.5 percentage points over three months signals imminent recessionary conditions, which depress corporate profits and produce negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by 0.5% or more over 3 months
failure_condition: In more than 30% of trigger events, 60-day forward returns are positive, or the average return over triggered periods does not differ significantly from zero.

mechanism_narrative: When both retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) rise month-over-month in the same month, it indicates synchronized strength in consumption and residential investment, boosting GDP expectations and S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint occurrence of positive RSAFS and HOUST changes does not yield positive median 20-day returns, or the signal's accuracy is below 55% over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle (federal funds rate above 3%), any further month-over-month increase in FEDFUNDS of at least 25 basis points reinforces restrictive policy, lowers equity prices, and generates negative S&P 500 returns within 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by 25bp or more in a month
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with FEDFUNDS above 3%
failure_condition: In tightening cycles, FEDFUNDS increase events lead to positive 20-day returns in more than 40% of occurrences, or the average excess return is positive.
